Accountant Job at Stridewaves Limited




        
    

        
        At stridewaves Limited you can optimise efficiency with our waste management solutions ensure quality supply and delivery of machinery and equipment, embrace renewable energy with our expert installation and supply of solar panels and inverters,streamline your operations through our reliable payment and personnel outsourcing services, rely on us for your wellhead maintenance and oil spillage clean-up. Elevate your business with our comprehensive services designed to optimise and innovate.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Job Summary
     

            
    




	Stridewaves Limited is seeking a qualified and detail-oriented Accountant to join our growing team in Port Harcourt.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s / HND in Accounting or related field.
	At least 2 years of relevant experience.
	Proficiency in accounting software and Microsoft Excel, strong analytical and communication skills.


Method of Application 
Interested candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using “Application for Accountant” as the subject of the mail.

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head, HR Operations",
  "description": "Egbin Power Plc. Lagos is one of the biggest additions to the electricity industry in Nigeria. Often seen as the biggest single generating power station in Sub-Saharan Africa, it was built to meet the every rising demand for electricity in Lagos and its environs.

Following the nation&rsquo;s privatization exercise, Sahara, through its power division, Sahara Power Group and sundry affiliations, acquired the 1320 MW installed capacity Egbin Power Plant. We are working towards deploying a minimum of 5,000 MW of electricity generation over the next five years.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, HR Operations

Job Identification: 598
Job Location: Ikorodu, Lagos

Job Description


	The Head, HR Operations, will lead the day-to-day employee-related activities to ensure an efficient, engaged, and productive workforce and contribute to the organisation&#39;s success.
	The role is responsible for overseeing the implementation of approved HR policies and processes related to employee requests, employee relations, and disciplinary matters, ensuring that all cases are handled promptly, consistently, transparently, and fairly for all stakeholders.


Key Duties and Responsibilities
HR Operations &amp; Policy Management:


	Ensure HR policies, procedures, and employee handbooks are readily accessible across the organisation and provide clarification to employees and managers as required.&nbsp;
	Oversee the consistent implementation of HR operational policies and ensure alignment across all divisions and locations.&nbsp;
	Provide guidance to HR Business Partners and HR representatives deployed across business units to ensure effective service delivery and compliance with company policies.&nbsp;


Employee Relations &amp; Industrial Relations:


	Manage the employee relations framework, ensuring fair, consistent, and transparent handling of grievances, disputes, and disciplinary cases.&nbsp;
	Oversee the disciplinary process and provide well-informed recommendations to management in line with company policy and labour regulations.&nbsp;
	Build and maintain strong relationships with employees, unions, and other stakeholders to proactively address concerns and foster a positive work environment.&nbsp;
	Stay abreast of labour laws, industrial relations trends, and regulatory changes, ensuring timely updates and compliance within the organisation.&nbsp;


HR Information Systems &amp; Data Management:


	Ensure accurate and timely capture of employee data in the HR Information System (HRIS).&nbsp;
	Collaborate with other HR functions to maintain data integrity, completeness, and consistency across HR systems.&nbsp;
	Conduct periodic audits to identify and resolve discrepancies in employee records and HR data.&nbsp;


Employee Lifecycle Management:


	Oversee leave management processes, ensuring effective tracking and continuity of business operations during employee absences.&nbsp;
	Manage the employee exit process, ensuring structured, compliant, and well-coordinated offboarding procedures.&nbsp;


Employee Engagement &amp; Communication:


	Oversee internal employee communication initiatives, including town halls, surveys, newsletters, focus groups, and one-on-one engagement sessions.&nbsp;
	Promote effective communication channels that support employee engagement and organisational alignment.&nbsp;


Health &amp; Employee Welfare Administration:


	Coordinate employee health-related programs, including healthcare service providers, HMOs, and wellness initiatives.&nbsp;
	Support health awareness and employee well-being programs across the organisation.&nbsp;


Reporting, Planning &amp; Budgeting:


	Provide periodic reports on HR Operations performance, trends, and key metrics to the Head of Human Resources.&nbsp;
	Prepare and manage the unit&rsquo;s budget inputs and contribute to the overall HR departmental budget planning process.&nbsp;
	Develop and implement annual HR Operations work plans aligned with organisational objectives.&nbsp;


Leadership &amp; Governance:


	Coach, mentor, and conduct performance evaluations for direct reports to support continuous development and performance improvement.&nbsp;
	Approve operational expenditures within delegated authority limits.&nbsp;
	Perform any other duties as assigned by the Head of Human Resources.&nbsp;


Compliance, Data Protection &amp; QHSE:


	Ensure compliance with Nigeria Data Protection Regulations (NDPR) and other applicable data protection laws and policies.&nbsp;
	Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) where necessary to mitigate risks in HR data processing.&nbsp;
	Ensure full adherence to Quality, Health, Safety, and Environment (QHSE) policies and integration of Integrated Management System (IMS) standards (ISO 9001:2015, ISO 14001:2015, ISO 45001:2018) into HR operations.&nbsp;
	Promote awareness of hazards, risks, and environmental impacts in relation to HR operational activities and ensure compliance within assigned responsibilities.


Education and Work Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Social Sciences, Humanities, or a related discipline
	Minimum of 10 years of relevant HR experience, with at least 3 years in a managerial HR Operations or HR Generalist leadership role, preferably within the power, energy, or industrial sector.
	Professional HR Certification (e.g, CIPM, SHRM, HRCI) is required.


Skills and Competencies
Technical Competencies:


	Strong understanding of HR operations, policies, and employee lifecycle management.&nbsp;
	Deep knowledge of employee and industrial relations practices.&nbsp;
	Working knowledge of labour laws and regulatory compliance frameworks.&nbsp;
	HR Information Systems (HRIS) and data management proficiency.
	Understanding of HR service delivery models and process optimisation.&nbsp;
	Budgeting and HR operational planning.


Leadership &amp; Behavioural Competencies:


	Strategic and systems thinking.&nbsp;
	Strong leadership and people management skills.
	Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.&nbsp;
	Strong negotiation and influencing abilities.&nbsp;
	High level of integrity and ethical standards.&nbsp;
	Strong problem-solving and conflict resolution skills.&nbsp;
	Excellent organisational and planning abilities.&nbsp;
	Business writing and presentation skills.
	Good administration and organisational skills.
	Strong leadership, relationship management and interpersonal skills.
	Very good communication and presentation skills.
	Advanced leadership and people management skills, including the ability to manage teams effectively.

Head, HR Operations Job at Egbin Power Plc




        
    

        
        Egbin Power Plc. Lagos is one of the biggest additions to the electricity industry in Nigeria. Often seen as the biggest single generating power station in Sub-Saharan Africa, it was built to meet the every rising demand for electricity in Lagos and its environs.

Following the nation’s privatization exercise, Sahara, through its power division, Sahara Power Group and sundry affiliations, acquired the 1320 MW installed capacity Egbin Power Plant. We are working towards deploying a minimum of 5,000 MW of electricity generation over the next five years.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, HR Operations

Job Identification: 598
Job Location: Ikorodu, Lagos
     

            
    



Job Description


	The Head, HR Operations, will lead the day-to-day employee-related activities to ensure an efficient, engaged, and productive workforce and contribute to the organisation's success.
	The role is responsible for overseeing the implementation of approved HR policies and processes related to employee requests, employee relations, and disciplinary matters, ensuring that all cases are handled promptly, consistently, transparently, and fairly for all stakeholders.


Key Duties and Responsibilities
HR Operations & Policy Management:


	Ensure HR policies, procedures, and employee handbooks are readily accessible across the organisation and provide clarification to employees and managers as required. 
	Oversee the consistent implementation of HR operational policies and ensure alignment across all divisions and locations. 
	Provide guidance to HR Business Partners and HR representatives deployed across business units to ensure effective service delivery and compliance with company policies. 


Employee Relations & Industrial Relations:


	Manage the employee relations framework, ensuring fair, consistent, and transparent handling of grievances, disputes, and disciplinary cases. 
	Oversee the disciplinary process and provide well-informed recommendations to management in line with company policy and labour regulations. 
	Build and maintain strong relationships with employees, unions, and other stakeholders to proactively address concerns and foster a positive work environment. 
	Stay abreast of labour laws, industrial relations trends, and regulatory changes, ensuring timely updates and compliance within the organisation. 


HR Information Systems & Data Management:


	Ensure accurate and timely capture of employee data in the HR Information System (HRIS). 
	Collaborate with other HR functions to maintain data integrity, completeness, and consistency across HR systems. 
	Conduct periodic audits to identify and resolve discrepancies in employee records and HR data. 


Employee Lifecycle Management:


	Oversee leave management processes, ensuring effective tracking and continuity of business operations during employee absences. 
	Manage the employee exit process, ensuring structured, compliant, and well-coordinated offboarding procedures. 


Employee Engagement & Communication:


	Oversee internal employee communication initiatives, including town halls, surveys, newsletters, focus groups, and one-on-one engagement sessions. 
	Promote effective communication channels that support employee engagement and organisational alignment. 


Health & Employee Welfare Administration:


	Coordinate employee health-related programs, including healthcare service providers, HMOs, and wellness initiatives. 
	Support health awareness and employee well-being programs across the organisation. 


Reporting, Planning & Budgeting:


	Provide periodic reports on HR Operations performance, trends, and key metrics to the Head of Human Resources. 
	Prepare and manage the unit’s budget inputs and contribute to the overall HR departmental budget planning process. 
	Develop and implement annual HR Operations work plans aligned with organisational objectives. 


Leadership & Governance:


	Coach, mentor, and conduct performance evaluations for direct reports to support continuous development and performance improvement. 
	Approve operational expenditures within delegated authority limits. 
	Perform any other duties as assigned by the Head of Human Resources. 


Compliance, Data Protection & QHSE:


	Ensure compliance with Nigeria Data Protection Regulations (NDPR) and other applicable data protection laws and policies. 
	Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) where necessary to mitigate risks in HR data processing. 
	Ensure full adherence to Quality, Health, Safety, and Environment (QHSE) policies and integration of Integrated Management System (IMS) standards (ISO 9001:2015, ISO 14001:2015, ISO 45001:2018) into HR operations. 
	Promote awareness of hazards, risks, and environmental impacts in relation to HR operational activities and ensure compliance within assigned responsibilities.


Education and Work Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Social Sciences, Humanities, or a related discipline
	Minimum of 10 years of relevant HR experience, with at least 3 years in a managerial HR Operations or HR Generalist leadership role, preferably within the power, energy, or industrial sector.
	Professional HR Certification (e.g, CIPM, SHRM, HRCI) is required.


Skills and Competencies
Technical Competencies:


	Strong understanding of HR operations, policies, and employee lifecycle management. 
	Deep knowledge of employee and industrial relations practices. 
	Working knowledge of labour laws and regulatory compliance frameworks. 
	HR Information Systems (HRIS) and data management proficiency.
	Understanding of HR service delivery models and process optimisation. 
	Budgeting and HR operational planning.


Leadership & Behavioural Competencies:


	Strategic and systems thinking. 
	Strong leadership and people management skills.
	Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills. 
	Strong negotiation and influencing abilities. 
	High level of integrity and ethical standards. 
	Strong problem-solving and conflict resolution skills. 
	Excellent organisational and planning abilities. 
	Business writing and presentation skills.
	Good administration and organisational skills.
	Strong leadership, relationship management and interpersonal skills.
	Very good communication and presentation skills.
	Advanced leadership and people management skills, including the ability to manage teams effectively.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Offshore Construction Manager",
  "description": "Padua Petroleum Nigeria Limited is an indigenous company established to provide a unique range of services to the Nigerian Oil and Gas industry.

We venture into the industry with the objective of building on existing limited in-country capacity. In view of the enormous opportunities which abound in the Oil and Gas industry in Nigeria and the new findings in other African countries, there is need for more indigenous players to fill the yawning gap by providing competitiveness and quality service delivery to the industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Offshore Construction Manager

job Location: Nigeria

Job Summary


	The Offshore Construction Manager is responsible for the planning, coordination, management, and execution of offshore construction activities associated with offshore oil and gas developments.
	The role provides strategic leadership and technical oversight for offshore installation, subsea construction, SURF activities, marine operations, hook-up, commissioning support, and brownfield modifications, ensuring projects are executed safely, efficiently, on schedule, within budget, and in compliance with contractual, regulatory, and company requirements.


Key Responsibilities
Offshore Construction Leadership:


	Lead and manage all offshore construction activities from project planning through execution and project close-out.
	Provide overall leadership for offshore construction teams, contractors, subcontractors, and vessel personnel.
	Develop offshore construction execution strategies, schedules, work plans, and resource requirements.
	Ensure construction activities are executed in accordance with approved procedures, project specifications, and client requirements.
	Coordinate multidisciplinary teams to achieve project objectives relating to safety, quality, cost, and schedule.


Construction Planning and Execution:


	Participate in project development, constructability reviews, installation studies, and execution planning.
	Review and approve offshore construction procedures, method statements, risk assessments, lifting plans, and marine execution plans.
	Manage offshore construction activities involving subsea structures and manifolds, pipelines, flowlines, and risers, umbilicals and tie-in systems, jackets and topsides, mooring systems, brownfield modifications, hook-up and commissioning support, and Inspection, Repair, and Maintenance (IRM) campaigns.
	Coordinate fabrication interfaces, loadout activities, transportation, installation, and commissioning support.


Offshore Operations and Marine Coordination:


	Oversee offshore construction vessels, barges, heavy-lift vessels, pipelay vessels, diving support vessels, and marine support craft.
	Coordinate offshore logistics, personnel movements, equipment mobilization, and material management.
	Interface with Marine Warranty Surveyors, vessel owners, marine authorities, and client representatives.
	Ensure effective coordination between offshore construction, marine, engineering, and commissioning teams.
	Monitor offshore progress and implement corrective actions to address schedule or operational challenges.


Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) Responsibilities:


	Promote and maintain a strong safety culture across all offshore construction activities.
	Ensure compliance with company HSE policies, client requirements, and applicable regulatory standards.
	Lead HAZID, HAZOP, SIMOPS, JSA, and operational risk assessment activities.
	Ensure permit-to-work systems and safe work practices are effectively implemented.
	Lead investigations into incidents, near misses, and non-conformances and ensure corrective actions are implemented.
	Monitor environmental compliance during offshore operations.


Quality and Project Assurance:


	Ensure compliance with project quality requirements, inspection plans, and construction specifications.
	Participate in audits, project assurance reviews, and regulatory inspections.
	Monitor contractor quality performance and construction compliance.
	Support mechanical completion, pre-commissioning, commissioning, and handover activities.
	Review and approve construction reports, progress updates, and close-out documentation.


Commercial and Stakeholder Management:


	Support contract administration, change management, and claims evaluation activities.
	Monitor project budgets, resource utilization, productivity, and cost performance.
	Participate in project reviews, management meetings, and client engagements.
	Provide regular reports on project status, risks, opportunities, and performance metrics.
	Manage relationships with clients, contractors, suppliers, and regulatory bodies.


Knowledge Transfer and Team Development:


	Mentor and develop local construction engineers, supervisors, and offshore personnel.
	Lead structured knowledge transfer and competency development initiatives.
	Support succession planning and local content objectives.
	Promote continuous improvement and lessons-learned programs across project teams.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Marine Engineering, Ocean Engineering, Petroleum Engineering, Naval Architecture, or a related engineering discipline.
	Master&#39;s Degree in Engineering, Project Management, Construction Management, or Business Administration is advantageous.
	Professional Engineering Registration or membership of a recognized engineering institution is preferred.


Experience:


	Minimum of 10 years of experience in offshore construction, offshore installation, marine operations, or project execution within the oil and gas industry.
	Minimum of 10 years&#39; experience in senior offshore construction management, offshore installation management, project management, or construction leadership roles.
	Extensive experience in offshore construction involving SURF systems, subsea structures, pipelines, offshore platforms, and marine operations.
	Proven experience managing major EPCI, offshore construction, and deepwater development projects.
	Extensive international offshore project experience is highly preferred.
	Demonstrated experience managing multidisciplinary teams and complex offshore execution campaigns.


Required Certifications:


	BOSIET/FOET with CA-EBS.
	HUET Certification.
	Offshore Medical Certificate.
	HAZID/HAZOP and Risk Assessment Training.
	Incident Investigation Training.
	Project Management Certification (PMP, PRINCE2, or equivalent) is advantageous.
	Any client-specific or project-required certifications.


Technical Skills:


	Expert knowledge of offshore construction methodologies, subsea installation techniques, and marine operations.
	Extensive experience in SURF projects, pipeline installation, heavy lifting, offshore structures, and hook-up activities.
	Strong understanding of offshore logistics, marine assurance, vessel operations, and project execution.
	Familiarity with API, DNV, ISO, IMCA, IOGP, and international offshore construction standards.
	Ability to review engineering drawings, installation procedures, risk assessments, and project documentation.
	Strong project planning, execution, budget control, and resource management capabilities.
	Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, Primavera P6, MS Project, and project reporting systems.


Competencies:


	Strong leadership and team management capabilities.
	Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
	Strong analytical, commercial, and decision-making abilities.
	Effective planning, coordination, and organizational skills.
	Ability to manage complex offshore construction operations under demanding conditions.
	Strong commitment to safety, quality, and operational excellence.
	Fluent in reading, writing, and speaking English.
	Ability to work effectively in multicultural and international project environments.


Preferred Additional Requirements:


	Experience on major deepwater and ultra-deepwater offshore field developments.
	Demonstrated expertise in offshore installation, SURF, subsea construction, and brownfield modification projects.
	Proven capability in mentoring and developing local engineering and offshore construction personnel.
	Strong understanding of offshore regulatory requirements and international industry best practices.
	Experience supporting commissioning, start-up, and operational readiness activities.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application to career@ppnl.com.ng and copying stakeholder.recruitment@ncdmb.gov.ng, using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Investment Marketer",
  "description": "Tempkers Limited - Our client, GABS Financial Services Limited, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job position: Investment Marketer

Job Location: Central Business District, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	They are seeking a results-driven and relationship-oriented Investment Marketer to drive the sales of our SEC-regulated investment products.
	The ideal candidate will identify and engage prospective investors, build lasting relationships with high-net-worth individuals (HNIs) and institutional clients, and contribute to the company&#39;s business growth through effective investment advisory and client acquisition.


Responsibilities
Business Development &amp; Client Acquisition:


	Market and sell GABS Financial Services&#39; SEC-regulated investment products to individual and institutional clients.
	Identify, prospect, and convert qualified leads into active investors.
	Develop and execute strategies to expand the company&#39;s investment portfolio and client base.
	Meet and exceed assigned sales and revenue targets.


Relationship Management:


	Build and maintain strong relationships with High-Net-Worth Individuals (HNIs), corporate organizations, and strategic partners.
	Deliver exceptional client service by understanding clients&#39; financial goals and recommending suitable investment solutions.
	Maintain regular communication with clients to strengthen relationships and encourage repeat investments.


Investment Advisory &amp; Product Presentation:


	Conduct investment presentations, seminars, and educational sessions to promote the company&#39;s investment offerings.
	Prepare and present compelling investment proposals, financial analyses, and product recommendations.
	Clearly communicate investment features, benefits, risks, and expected returns while ensuring regulatory compliance.


Market Intelligence &amp; Reporting:


	Monitor market trends and identify new business opportunities within the investment landscape.
	Prepare periodic sales, pipeline, and client engagement reports for management.
	Maintain accurate records of client interactions, investment activities, and business development efforts.


Collaboration &amp; Compliance:


	Work closely with internal teams to ensure seamless onboarding and servicing of clients.
	Ensure compliance with all regulatory requirements, internal policies, and ethical sales practices.
	Represent the company professionally while upholding its values of integrity, excellence, and client-centric service.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree (B.Sc. or equivalent) in any discipline.
	Minimum of 2&ndash;4 years of experience in investment marketing, banking, insurance, wealth management, or financial services.
	Demonstrated experience in investment sales and business development is highly preferred.
	Knowledge of SEC-regulated investment products will be an added advantage.
	Must be a resident of Abuja, Nigeria.


Skills &amp; Qualifications:

Investment Marketer Job at Tempkers Limited




        
    

        
        Tempkers Limited - Our client, GABS Financial Services Limited, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job position: Investment Marketer

Job Location: Central Business District, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	They are seeking a results-driven and relationship-oriented Investment Marketer to drive the sales of our SEC-regulated investment products.
	The ideal candidate will identify and engage prospective investors, build lasting relationships with high-net-worth individuals (HNIs) and institutional clients, and contribute to the company's business growth through effective investment advisory and client acquisition.


Responsibilities
Business Development & Client Acquisition:


	Market and sell GABS Financial Services' SEC-regulated investment products to individual and institutional clients.
	Identify, prospect, and convert qualified leads into active investors.
	Develop and execute strategies to expand the company's investment portfolio and client base.
	Meet and exceed assigned sales and revenue targets.


Relationship Management:


	Build and maintain strong relationships with High-Net-Worth Individuals (HNIs), corporate organizations, and strategic partners.
	Deliver exceptional client service by understanding clients' financial goals and recommending suitable investment solutions.
	Maintain regular communication with clients to strengthen relationships and encourage repeat investments.


Investment Advisory & Product Presentation:


	Conduct investment presentations, seminars, and educational sessions to promote the company's investment offerings.
	Prepare and present compelling investment proposals, financial analyses, and product recommendations.
	Clearly communicate investment features, benefits, risks, and expected returns while ensuring regulatory compliance.


Market Intelligence & Reporting:


	Monitor market trends and identify new business opportunities within the investment landscape.
	Prepare periodic sales, pipeline, and client engagement reports for management.
	Maintain accurate records of client interactions, investment activities, and business development efforts.


Collaboration & Compliance:


	Work closely with internal teams to ensure seamless onboarding and servicing of clients.
	Ensure compliance with all regulatory requirements, internal policies, and ethical sales practices.
	Represent the company professionally while upholding its values of integrity, excellence, and client-centric service.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree (B.Sc. or equivalent) in any discipline.
	Minimum of 2–4 years of experience in investment marketing, banking, insurance, wealth management, or financial services.
	Demonstrated experience in investment sales and business development is highly preferred.
	Knowledge of SEC-regulated investment products will be an added advantage.
	Must be a resident of Abuja, Nigeria.


Skills & Qualifications:


	Strong sales, negotiation, and business development skills.
	Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal abilities.
	Ability to build and sustain relationships with high-value clients.
	Strong analytical and financial advisory skills.
	Proven ability to achieve sales targets and drive revenue growth.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.
	Strong organizational, reporting, and time management skills.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team.


Desired Qualities:


	Results-oriented and self-motivated.
	Professional, confident, and persuasive.
	High level of integrity and ethical conduct.
	Strong networking and relationship-building abilities.
	Customer-focused with excellent problem-solving skills.
	Proactive, resilient, and target-driven.


Additional Information:


	This role is open to female candidates only.
	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.


Compensation & Benefits


	Salary: N250,000 per month.
	Pension Scheme.
	13th Month Allowance.
	Leave Allowance.
	Health Insurance.
	Professional development and career growth opportunities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Investment Marketer – Abuja" as the subject of the mail.

Note


	This role is open to female candidates only.
	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Telemarketer Job at Meritabode Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Meritabode Nigeria Limited is a Real Estate company situated and registered in Nigeria with the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) with registration number RC 1014354. We are dedicated to making a positive difference in the industry, which we have achieved by engaging qualified hands who are passionate about what they do and a project team that maintains a strong foundation of trust and mutual respect through positive relationships with clients and stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Telemarketer
Job Location: Opebi, Ikeja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Make outbound calls to prospective clients from provided databases and lead lists.
	Introduce and promote the company's products, services, and offers.
	Generate and qualify leads for the sales team.
	Schedule appointments and site inspections for prospective clients.
	Follow up on inquiries and maintain regular communication with prospects.
	Maintain accurate records of calls, customer interactions, and outcomes.
	Achieve daily, weekly, and monthly call and lead-generation targets.
	Handle customer inquiries professionally and escalate issues where necessary.
	Update customer information in the company's CRM or database.
	Prepare periodic reports on call activities and lead conversions.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an HND qualification with 2 - 4 years of experience. 


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
